UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores profile information for a user of the Maintain UK application. Authentication data is handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a single rental property managed within the Maintain UK application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns/manages this property. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Property)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the property's address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the property's address (optional).

city.description: The city where the property is located.

propertyType.description: The type of property (e.g., flat, house, bungalow, maisonette, studio, HMO).

status.description: The current status of the property (e.g., Vacant, Occupied, Under Maintenance).

numberOfBedrooms.description: The number of bedrooms in the property.

numberOfBathrooms.description: The number of bathrooms in the property.

notes.description: General notes or comments about the property.

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents a tenant associated with a rental property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property the tenant is occupying. (Relationship: Property 1:N Tenant)

firstName.description: The first name of the tenant.

lastName.description: The last name of the tenant.

tenancyStartDate.description: The start date of the tenancy agreement.

tenancyEndDate.description: The anticipated end date of the tenancy agreement.

notes.description: Specific notes about the tenant or tenancy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was last updated.

MaintenanceRequest.title: MaintenanceRequest
MaintenanceRequest.description: Represents a logged maintenance issue for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRequest entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property the maintenance request is for. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

reportedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who reported the maintenance request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

title.description: A brief title for the maintenance issue.

description.description: Detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgencyLevel.description: The urgency level of the request (e.g., Low, Medium, High, Emergency).

status.description: The current status of the request (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed, Pending Approval).

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request was reported.

resolvedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request was resolved (optional).

notes.description: Additional notes or updates on the maintenance request.

assignedTo.description: Name or identifier of the person/vendor assigned to the task.

cost.description: Estimated or actual cost of the maintenance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property the inspection is for. (Relationship: Property 1:N Inspection)

scheduledByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who scheduled the inspection. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Inspection)

title.description: A title for the inspection (e.g., 'Quarterly Check').

description.description: Details about the inspection purpose or scope.

scheduledDateTime.description: The date and time when the inspection is scheduled.

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled).

outcomeNotes.description: Notes on the outcome of the inspection after completion.

nextInspectionDate.description: Suggested date for the next inspection.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document uploaded and associated with a property or tenant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this document is associated with. (Relationship: Property 1:N Document)

tenantId.description: Reference to the Tenant this document is associated with (optional). (Relationship: Tenant 0:N Document)

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded the document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type or extension of the file (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg').

fileUrl.description: The URL where the document is stored.

documentType.description: Categorization of the document (e.g., 'Tenancy Agreement', 'EPC', 'Gas Safety Certificate', 'Property Manual').

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

expirationDate.description: Optional expiration date for documents like certificates.

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Records various activities within the application for display in a recent activity feed and for auditing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed the action. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ActivityLog)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property related to this activity (optional, but likely present for most activities). (Relationship: Property 0:N ActivityLog)

activityType.description: The type of action performed (e.g., 'Property Added', 'Maintenance Logged', 'Inspection Scheduled', 'Document Uploaded', 'Tenant Added').

description.description: A detailed description of the activity.

timestamp.description: The exact date and time the activity occurred.

relatedEntityId.description: ID of the entity primarily affected by this activity (e.g., Property ID, MaintenanceRequest ID, Inspection ID, Document ID).

relatedEntityType.description: Type of the entity primarily affected (e.g., 'Property', 'MaintenanceRequest', 'Inspection', 'Document', 'Tenant').

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Access is restricted to the owner matching the '{userId}' path parameter. The 'id' field in the document must match '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Stores properties owned by a specific user. The 'ownerId' field in the document must match '{userId}' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific property.

definition.description: Stores tenant information for a specific property. Documents MUST include a denormalized 'propertyOwnerId' field (matching '{userId}' from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tenant.

definition.description: Stores maintenance requests for a specific property. Documents MUST include a denormalized 'propertyOwnerId' field (matching '{userId}' from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: maintenanceRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific maintenance request.

definition.description: Stores inspection records for a specific property. Documents MUST include a denormalized 'propertyOwnerId' field (matching '{userId}' from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific inspection.

definition.description: Stores documents associated with a specific property. Documents MUST include a denormalized 'propertyOwnerId' field (matching '{userId}' from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property owner, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent property.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific document.

definition.description: Stores activity logs for a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document must match '{userId}' for authorization independence and secure QAPs.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose activity is logged, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: activityLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific activity log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access by strictly adhering to the core design principles:

1.  **Authorization Independence via Denormalization:** This is the cornerstone of the design. For every subcollection document (e.g., `Tenant`, `MaintenanceRequest`, `Inspection`, `Document`) whose access is tied to a parent `Property`, the `propertyOwnerId` (the ID of the `UserProfile` who owns the parent `Property`) is explicitly denormalized and stored within each subcollection document. This eliminates the need for expensive and rule-breaking `get()` calls to parent documents for authorization checks. For instance, to check if a user can access a `MaintenanceRequest`, the rules will simply verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.propertyOwnerId`, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic.

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** All documents within a given collection path share the same security requirements. For example, all `Property` documents under `/users/{userId}/properties` are owned by `{userId}`. This prevents mixing different access levels within the same collection, making security rules straightforward and easier to reason about.

3.  **Access Modeling & Hierarchical Paths:** The structure largely employs path-based ownership for user-specific and user-owned data. User profiles are stored at `/userProfiles/{userId}`. All entities belonging to a user (like `Properties`) or nested under a user's owned entity (like `Tenants`, `MaintenanceRequests`, `Inspections`, `Documents` under a `Property`) follow a consistent hierarchical pattern: `/users/{userId}/properties/{propertyId}/<subcollection>/{docId}`. This makes ownership explicit and naturally aligns with `request.auth.uid` for authorization.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The hierarchical path structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. When a user queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/properties` or any of its subcollections (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/properties/{propertyId}/maintenanceRequests`), the query path itself constrains the results to only the data owned by the authenticated user. Combined with the denormalized `propertyOwnerId` in subcollection documents (e.g., `resource.data.propertyOwnerId == request.auth.uid`), security rules can efficiently ensure that only authorized data is returned, preventing accidental data exposure and supporting robust client-side queries without relying on client-enforced filtering. The `ActivityLog` also follows this for user-specific activity, providing secure `list` for recent activities at `/users/{userId}/activityLogs`.

5.  **Data Clarity and Predictability:** Semantic naming conventions are used for paths and parameters (e.g., `{userId}`, `{propertyId}`). Standard fields like `ownerId`, `propertyOwnerId`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, and `status` ensure a predictable schema and clear intent. The `status` field in `Property`, `MaintenanceRequest`, and `Inspection` explicitly models the finite states of these entities.
